items.title: Thai cabinet approves plans to borrow $12bn to ease Iran war fallout
Two soldiers hurt in Hezbollah drone attack in south Lebanon; IDF hits terror sites
items.description: Home of south Lebanon mayor reportedly hit as IDF warned 12 villages to evacuate; military says 25 Hezbollah targets hit in the past day, including weapon depots The post Two soldiers hurt in Hezbollah drone attack in south Lebanon; IDF hits terror sites appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: Myanmar: What's behind Suu Kyi's transfer to house arrest?
items.description: The transfer of Aung San Suu Kyi from prison to house arrest has been welcomed cautiously abroad, but experts say Myanmar's military may be using it to boost legitimacy as the nation remains engulfed in civil war.

items.title: War in Ukraine: Russia attacks east and south Ukraine with over 100 drones
items.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Wednesday that Russia had decided to reject efforts to halt fighting and save lives by launching fresh attacks on Ukraine, which had called a unilateral ceasefire.

items.title: What is the FDD, the pro-Israel think tank shaping Trump’s Iran policy?
items.description: FDD's talking points on the US-Israel war on Iran have been picked up by the Trump administration.

items.title: Robot wars - what an operation in Ukraine tells us about the battlefield of the near future
items.description: After Ukraine's President Zelensky said territory had been captured using just robots and drones, what is the future of unmanned warfare?

items.title: Zelensky, 'Mosca ha violato tregua, agiremo di conseguenza'
items.description: 'Ovvio che una guerra in corso non è il momento giusto per celebrazioni pubbliche'

items.title: ‘Putin only cares about parades’: fury as Russia rains missiles on Ukraine during 24-hour truce
items.description: Dozens killed despite ceasefire announced by Zelenskyy, after Moscow asked for Saturday truce for its annual military paradeEurope live – latest updatesKyiv has criticised Russia for attacking several Ukrainian cities overnight with more than 100 combat drones and three missiles, in spite of a unilateral 24-hour ceasef…

items.title: Skeletons in their clothing: Recovering bodies from the rubble in Gaza
items.description: It was one of the deadliest Israeli strikes of the Gaza war, devastating one extended family. A year and a half later, the survivors find their loved ones' remains.

items.title: Russian Strikes Kill 22 in Ukraine Hours Before Kyiv-Proposed Ceasefire
items.description: The strikes came hours before a proposed ceasefire, with Zelensky condemning Russia’s “utter cynicism” in launching deadly attacks while seeking a brief truce for its May 9 Victory Day parade.

items.title: Kyiv's unilateral ceasefire already broken by Russian strikes
items.description: At least 28 people were killed in Russian strikes just before Ukraine's ceasefire was due to take effect on Wednesday, even as Kyiv accused Moscow of new violations hours later.

items.title: UAE Accuses Iran Of Hitting Oil Sector, World Leaders Condemn Dangerous Escalation
items.description: Several countries have condemned Iran and called an attack on oil facilities in the United Arab Emirates a dangerous escalation. Thick smoke was filmed pouring out from the Fujairah Oil Industry Zone that the UAE claimed was hit by an Iranian drone attack on May 4. This comes as both the US and Iran fired shots in the …

items.title: How Colombia became a top exporter of mercenaries
items.description: Every year Colombia retires thousands of battle-hardened veterans from its armed forces. Many end up enlisting in foreign conflicts.

items.title: Education or food? Funding shortfalls force Sudanese refugees in Egypt to ‘make very difficult decisions’
items.description: Civilians who have fled the war in Sudan and sought shelter in neighbouring Egypt could potentially face a new battle – the loss of critical services that ensure their survival, the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, has warned.

items.title: U.K. Targets Russian Army Recruitment Network and Drone Suppliers in Latest Sanctions Package
items.description: The new measures target what British officials condemned as a “barbaric” network that lures foreign nationals to Russia with the promise of work, only to force them into frontline combat in Ukraine or military production.
